content:
https://github.com/fishinabarrel/linux-kernel-module-rust/
i followed the steps of the kernel_rust above , but it shows :
error: failed to run rustc to learn about target-specific information

Caused by:
process didn't exit successfully: rustc - --crate-name ___ --print=file-names --target x86_64-linux-kernel --crate-type bin --crate-type rlib --crate-type dylib --crate-type cdylib --crate-type staticlib --crate-type proc-macro --print=sysroot --print=split-debuginfo --print=crate-name --print=cfg (exit status: 1)
--- stderr
error: Error loading target specification: Could not find specification for target "x86_64-linux-kernel". Run rustc --print target-list for a list of built-in targets】

how to make a kernel module with rust in Ubuntu20.04?
That repository is no longer maintained, it's a predecessor for https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/

I encountered the same issue today but for me I was trying to run 'runtest' instead.
I saw from this comment [0], that split-debuginfo was introduced in rust 1.67.0 while the kernel currently requires rust 1.66.0. I worked around the issue by using rust-next and applying the following patch [1] to upgrade rust to 1.68.2

[0] https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/108343#issuecomment-1440280197
[1] https://lore.kernel.org/all/20230419133800.0da0857a.gary@garyguo.net/
